content.text:
IS Audit and Control
Semester:
3  |
Type:
UEM3  |
VHS:
42h  |
Weekly hours:
C: 1h30 / TD: 1h30  |
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
2  |
Credits:
4
Teaching Objectives
This module provides basic notions on auditing and the general audit process, and in particular IS auditing in its strategic, tactical and operational dimensions, as well as the use of audit tools, reference frameworks and methods.
Recommended prior knowledge:
Information systems, quality assurance.
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Introduction to IS Audit and Control
Definition and objectives of IS auditing
Importance of auditing in risk management and compliance
Regulatory framework and applicable standards (ISO, COBIT, etc.)
Types of audit (internal, external, suppliers)
Roles and responsibilities of actors involved
Planning and Preparation of an Audit Mission
Defining the objective and scope of the audit
Identifying information assets and associated risks
Developing the audit plan and procedures
Building the audit team and distributing tasks
Planning preparation meetings and interviews
IS Audit Conduct Process
Collection and analysis of information (interviews, documents, tests)
Evaluation of existing controls and their effectiveness
Identification of weaknesses, non-conformities and potential risks
Testing of IT controls and result analysis
Writing finding sheets and recommendations
Risk and Control Evaluation
Identification and analysis of IS/IT risks (cyber threats, human errors, etc.)
Impact and probability assessment
Analysis of relevance and effectiveness of existing controls
Risk mitigation plan proposal
Communication and Follow-Up of Results
Writing the audit report
Presenting results to governance bodies and stakeholders
Follow-up on recommendation implementation
Evaluating corrective action effectiveness
Specific Audit Aspects
Security and data protection audit
IS development and maintenance processes audit
Access and rights management audit
Regulatory compliance audit
Integrated systems audit (ERP, CRM, etc.)
Tools and Methodologies
Use of audit software and tools
ISO 27001, 27005 standards
Application of audit reference frameworks (COBIT, ITIL, CMMI, etc.)
Application of audit methods (MEHARI, EBIOS, …)
Collaboration with internal and external experts
Adapting the audit approach to organisational specificities
Assessment
In-person semester exam (60%)
Continuous assessment (CC) (40%): continuous tests, personal work
References
P. Jouffroy (2010).
COBIT : pour une meilleure gouvernance des systèmes d'information
. 2ème édition, Eyrolles.
G. Balantzian (2007).
Le Plan de Gouvernance du S.I.
Dunod.
C. Dumond (2007).
ITIL, pour un service informatique optimal
. Eyrolles.
M. Thorin (2000).
Audit informatique
. Hermès.
ISACA: www.isaca.org | ITIL: www.itil-officialsite.com | CMMI: www.sei.cmu.edu/cmmi/
